opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: A few hours ago the vision in my right eye went blurry and my cheek has pins and needles — I thought I was having a stroke.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly this evening and hasn't gone away; I'm scared.

symptom_narrative.character: blurring in the centre of the right eye, dull ache behind it when I move it, and tingling in my cheek
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: vision noticeably worse in that eye
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on over an hour or two, still present
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no arm or leg weakness, no slurred speech, no severe headache, no unsteadiness
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: struggling to read with the right eye

objective_data_offered.value: when I cover my left eye, the right eye's central vision is blurry and colours look washed out; covering the right eye, the left is normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-test; cannot confirm a relative afferent pupillary defect or formal acuity remotely
